day_summary: Tonight's game reports are ready - 4 games. Nothing needs you right now.

agent_updates.title: Blowout Error Pocket Investigated (Unfixable Ex-Ante)
agent_updates.description: Explored fixes for the blowout_12+ error pocket (our biggest MAE drag at 17.6 points). Tested inflating extreme spread predictions and variances ex-ante. Conclusion: Unfixable. Blowout status is a realized post-hoc slice. Applying asymmetric adjustments beforehand degraded the overall MAE and CRPS by over-correcting close games. Walked forward the hypothesis in fr2_blowout_adjustment.py and marked the task as investigated/unfixable.

agent_updates.title: Rebound Matchup Adjustments Implemented
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The Finding:** We were missing the mark on rebounds because we were completely ignoring head-to-head size differences and who is actually boxing out who.

**The Fix:** I built the **Rebound Matchup Model**. It uses the new matchups feed feed to dynamically fetch the expected defender for every player on the slate. It scales each player's proj_reb based on two things: 1) the defender's historical rebound rate, and 2) the height advantage/disadvantage (in inches).

**Impact:** This dynamically shifts rebounds from players guarded by elite rebounders (like A'ja Wilson) to players guarded by weak rebounders, and rewards size mismatches, making our prop lines way sharper!

agent_updates.title: Minutes Model Finalized: v8 beats v9 on Role-Change
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **The finding:** We judged the v9 (role-conditional) vs v8 (rolling) minutes models on the games that matter most: players stepping into bigger or different roles (the "tail slice").

**Result:** v8 is **BETTER by 0.86 MAE** on these role-change games. The v9 slot-delta adjustment systematically over-corrected on promoted/demoted players.

**Action:** We have permanently shelved the v9 slot-delta approach (Branch 2: Honest Null) and promoted v8 as the official minutes model.

agent_updates.title: Historical Era Adjustments (2024-2026)
agent_updates.body_markdown:
> **2026 season** represents a distinct era in WNBA officiating. Free Throw Rate skyrocketed from ~0.267 to **0.315**, adding over 3 free throw attempts per team per game.

**Impact on Backtesting:** When backtesting player stat projections (especially PTS) on 2024/2025 data, we must apply a downward era-adjustment for free throw volume to avoid artificially grading our 2026-calibrated models too harshly on historical undershoots.
